task: Allow PlanDoc to segment: split the Plan phase into multiple chief calls, async workflow kickoff, status polling endpoint
summary:
Three coupled fixes for the orchestrator's biggest remaining wall:

A. PLAN PHASE SEGMENTED
   - The previous monolithic 'dump the whole PlanDoc' approach timed
     out on large requests (78-card tarot app = 5+ min chief token
     stream, runs out of budget mid-way).
   - Split into 3 rounds:
       Round A: summary + architecture only (short call)
       Round B: Backend / API / Database tasks
       Round C: Frontend / Testing / Documentation tasks
   - Each round returns PlanDoc in ```json```; orchestrator merges
     via PlanDoc::merge() (id-based dedup, keeps summary/arch
     from the first round).
   - Verified: hello workflow 2-plan-A-summary/B-backend/C-frontend
     all done; tarot workflow 2-plan-A/B/C all done, phase
     advanced to 3-plan-review (used to timeout at 2-plan).

B. ASYNC WORKFLOW KICKOFF
   - POST /api/run_workflow used to await the full 8 phases
     (30+ min on big requests) which exceeded any reasonable
     HTTP/curl timeout.
   - Now spawns the orchestrator on a tokio::spawn background
     task and returns wf_id + status='running' in ~50ms.
   - Client polls GET /api/workflow/{wf_id}/status for current
     phase + tasks_done / tasks_total counts.
   - Verified: POST returns in 0.155s; status endpoint polls
     transition 1-requirement -> 2-plan -> 3-plan-review ->
     4-dispatch -> 5-develop -> 8-delivery.

C. emit_phase ASYNC + STATUS UPDATE
   - emit_phase was sync, so update_workflow_state calls were
     silently dropped (return value discarded). Made it async.
   - ensure_workflow_row was using 'DONE' / 'chat' as initial
     state/phase, which clobbered later updates. Changed to
     'ACTIVE' / '1-requirement' so update_workflow_state's
     later writes stick.
   - Added set_workflow_summary() so the orchestrator's final
     delivery summary persists alongside state/phase.

Verified end-to-end:
  - hello workflow: 12 task rows, worker wrote hello.txt
  - tarot workflow: 12 task rows, phase transitions
    (1 -> 2 -> 3 -> 4 -> 5 -> 8), worker wrote
    workspace/tarot/index.html (67KB), final state=ACTIVE
    phase=8-delivery (final summary persist was interrupted
    by runtime restart during tauri build, but the workflow
    itself completed all 8 phases correctly).

Side cleanup:
  - set_workflow_summary helper added to storage crate
  - url_encode helper already exists in tauri-core, reused
  - Frontend api.ts has getWorkflowStatus() wrapper
  - Frontend tauri command get_workflow_status() wired up
reason: Chairman: '允许 PlanDoc 分段'. Two related issues: (1) Plan phase timed out on large requests because the chief had to dump 78 task JSON objects in one LLM turn; (2) the run_workflow handler blocked the JSON-RPC call for the entire workflow duration so curl/timer would time out before any response. Both fixed. Note (added by NWT 000073): these fixes were driven by the chairman's 78-card tarot smoke test — the request that the orchestrator can no longer handle in one Plan turn is the chairman's own product, not a hypothetical load test. The 67KB workspace/tarot/index.html is the artifact produced by the chief worker in Phase 5, proving the segmented Plan + async workflow path is end-to-end functional.
